title: organization — validation rules
description: Custom Laravel validation rules published by OrganizationServiceProvider::boot() so any FormRequest / Data class can validate Organization writes. All rules can be used inline via the string signature OR as class instances for composition.

rules.name: valid_hex_color
rules.description: Enforces a strict 7-character hex color format (`#RRGGBB`). The `#` is required. Uppercase or lowercase A-F. Applied to primary_color + secondary_color on CreateOrganizationAction + UpdateOrganizationAction. Refuses short-form `#RGB` (visually the same but harder to compare programmatically).
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a 7-character hex color starting with `#` (e.g. `#1F2937`).

rules.name: valid_e164_phone
rules.description: Enforces ITU-T E.164 phone number format: `+` followed by 1..15 digits, no whitespace or punctuation. Applied to support_phone. Refuses formats like `(415) 555-1234` — callers must normalise before submitting.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be an E.164 phone number (e.g. `+14155551234`).

rules.name: valid_url
rules.description: Enforces full URL with scheme (defaults to allowing http + https). Rejects protocol-relative URLs and bare hostnames. Applied to website_url + logo_url.

params.name: scheme
params.description: Comma-joined allowed schemes. For logo_url the recommended usage is `valid_url:https` (force TLS).

rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a valid URL with an allowed scheme.

rules.name: parent_same_tenant
rules.description: Refuses when the proposed parent_id references an Organization belonging to a different tenant. Enforced on both CreateOrganizationAction (initial parent_id) and ReparentOrganizationAction (parent_id change). Defense-in-depth on top of the tenant scope.
rules.default_message: The parent organization must belong to the same tenant.

rules.name: parent_no_cycle
rules.description: Cycle-detection rule. Given the current Organization id + proposed parent_id, walks the proposed parent's ancestor chain (via existing tree_path) looking for the current id. Returns the cycle_path array in the validation error for UI visualisation. Applied on ReparentOrganizationAction (create-time parent_id is trivially cycle-safe because the new row has no descendants yet).

params.name: organization_id
params.description: ULID of the row whose parent is changing. Provided as `{organization}` from the route URL parameter.

rules.default_message: This reparent would create a cycle.

rules.name: parent_within_depth_cap
rules.description: Refuses when the proposed parent's tree_depth + 1 + (moved node's max descendant depth) would exceed config('organization.hierarchy.max_depth'). Applied on both create (moved node's descendant depth = 0) and reparent (moved node may carry a deep subtree).

params.name: organization_id
params.description: ULID of the row being moved (null on create — computes with descendant depth 0).

rules.default_message: This reparent exceeds the maximum tree depth of :max.

rules.name: organization_slug_reserved
rules.description: Refuses slugs listed in config('organization.validation.reserved_slugs'). The `allow_system` param bypasses the check when the writer is in system context (e.g. SeedDefaultOrganizationForTenantJob writing slug='default').

params.name: allow_system
params.description: When true, the seeder path is allowed to use reserved slugs. Kept off for API callers.

rules.default_message: The :attribute uses a reserved slug (:reserved). Choose a different value.

usage_from_form_request: The CreateOrganizationAction's Data class composes these rules automatically. Most callers never write these rules manually. This file is the canonical registry for the underlying rule implementations.
